items.title: From "napkin math" to turbopuffer
items.description:
The benefits of longer tenure, using first principles to build durable software – and why founders should be cautious when raising VC money. turbopuffer CEO Simon Eskildsen at AI Engineer's World Fair, in San Francisco, in July 2026, interviewed by Gergely Orosz.

See the accompanying The Pragmatic Engineer deepdive: Pushing software engineering limits with “napkin math” at https://newsletter.pragmaticengineer.com/p/pushing-software-engineering-limits

Timestamps:

00:00 Intro
02:00 Competitive programming
05:18 Joining Shopify
08:28 Infra at Shopify
10:59 toxiproxy
15:30 The Napkin Math project
20:00 Starting turbopuffer
27:56 How Cursor became turbopuffer's first customer
34:30 Asking Jensen Huang if he vapes
37:51 Why CPUs are suddenly scarce in the cloud
42:31 Simon's VC philosophy
48:30 Six reasons to raise VC money
51:00 Running a fully remote company: campfires and turbocredits

items.title: The creator of Zig is in shambles
items.description:
👉Join the Rust Live Accelerator waitlist: https://letsgetrusty.com/join/wv-1AsKx9fL8is

Bun's massive rewrite from Zig to Rust using Claude Code has sparked one of the biggest debates in systems programming. In this video, we break down why Bun made the switch, why Zig's creator publicly pushed back, and what it all means for Rust, Zig, AI-assisted programming, and the future of software engineering. Was this a marketing stunt, a technical success, or the beginning of a much bigger trend? Let me know your thoughts in the comments.
